fields.name: device_id
fields.description: Fixed device ID code of 0xE5 (11100101 binary / 345 octal).

fields.name: thresh_tap
fields.description: Tap threshold. The data format is unsigned, so the magnitude of the tap event is compared with the value in THRESH_TAP. The scale factor is 62.5 mg/LSB (that is, 0xFF = +16 g). A value of 0 may result in undesirable behavior if tap/double tap interrupts are enabled.

fields.name: ofsx
fields.description: X-axis offset adjustment in twos complement format with a scale factor of 15.6 mg/LSB (0x7F = +2 g).

fields.name: ofsy
fields.description: Y-axis offset adjustment in twos complement format with a scale factor of 15.6 mg/LSB (0x7F = +2 g)

fields.name: z_axis_offset
fields.description: User-set offset adjustment in twos complement format with a scale factor of 15.6 mg/LSB (0x7F = +2 g).

fields.name: dur
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the maximum time that an event must be above the THRESH_TAP threshold to qualify as a tap event. The scale factor is 625 μs/LSB. A value of 0 disables the tap/double tap functions.

fields.name: latent
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the wait time from the detection of a tap event to the start of the time window during which a possible second tap event can be detected. Scale factor is 1.25 ms/LSB. A value of 0 disables the double tap function.

fields.name: window
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the amount of time after the expiration of the latency time during which a second valid tap can begin. Scale factor: 1.25 ms/LSB. A value of 0 disables the double tap function.

fields.name: thresh_act
fields.description: Threshold value for detecting activity. Unsigned data format, scale factor 62.5 mg/LSB. Magnitude of activity event compared to this value.

fields.name: thresh_inact
fields.description: Holds the threshold value for detecting inactivity. The data format is unsigned, so the magnitude of the inactivity event is compared with the value in the THRESH_INACT register. The scale factor is 62.5 mg/LSB. A value of 0 mg may result in undesirable behavior if the inactivity interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: time_inact
fields.description: Unsigned 8-bit value representing the amount of time that acceleration must be less than THRESH_INACT for inactivity detection. Scale factor: 1 sec/LSB. Maximum value: 255 sec. Uses filtered output data.

fields.name: act_acdc
fields.description: ACT AC/DC bit. A setting of 0 selects dc-coupled operation, and a setting of 1 enables ac-coupled operation.

fields.name: act_x_en
fields.description: ACT_X enable bit. A setting of 1 enables x-axis participation in detecting activity. A setting of 0 excludes the x-axis from participation.

fields.name: act_y_en
fields.description: ACT_Y enable bit. A setting of 1 enables y-axis participation in detecting activity. A setting of 0 excludes the y-axis from participation.

fields.name: act_z_en
fields.description: ACT_Z enable bit. A setting of 1 enables z-axis participation in detecting activity. A setting of 0 excludes the z-axis from participation.

fields.name: inact_acdc
fields.description: INACT AC/DC bit. A setting of 0 selects dc-coupled operation, and a setting of 1 enables ac-coupled operation.

fields.name: inact_x_en
fields.description: INACT_X enable bit. A setting of 1 enables x-axis participation in detecting inactivity. A setting of 0 excludes the x-axis from participation.

fields.name: inact_y_en
fields.description: INACT_Y enable bit. A setting of 1 enables y-axis participation in detecting inactivity. A setting of 0 excludes the y-axis from participation.

fields.name: inact_z_en
fields.description: INACT_Z enable bit. A setting of 1 enables z-axis participation in detecting inactivity. A setting of 0 excludes the z-axis from participation. If all axes are excluded, the function is disabled.

fields.name: thresh_ff
fields.description: Free-fall threshold value, unsigned format. The root-sum-square (RSS) value of all axes is compared with this value. Scale factor is 62.5 mg/LSB. Recommended values: 0x05 to 0x09 (300 mg to 600 mg). A value of 0 may result in undesirable behavior if free-fall interrupt is enabled.

fields.name: time_ff
fields.description: Unsigned time value representing the minimum time that the RSS value of all axes must be less than THRESH_FF to generate a free-fall interrupt. The scale factor is 5 ms/LSB.

fields.name: suppress
fields.description: Setting the suppress bit suppresses double tap detection if acceleration greater than the value in THRESH_TAP is present between taps. See the Tap Detection section for more details.

fields.name: tap_x_enable
fields.description: A setting of 1 in the TAP_X enable bit enables x-axis participation in tap detection. A setting of 0 excludes the selected axis from participation in tap detection.

fields.name: tap_y_enable
fields.description: A setting of 1 in the TAP_Y enable bit enables y-axis participation in tap detection. A setting of 0 excludes the selected axis from participation in tap detection.

fields.name: tap_z_enable
fields.description: A setting of 1 in the TAP_Z enable bit enables z-axis participation in tap detection. A setting of 0 excludes the selected axis from participation in tap detection.

fields.name: tap_z_source
fields.description: TAP Z source bit. Indicates Z-axis involvement in tap event (1 = involved, 0 = not). Overwritten by new data.

fields.name: tap_y_source
fields.description: TAP Y source bit. Indicates Y-axis involvement in tap event (1 = involved, 0 = not). Overwritten by new data.

fields.name: tap_x_source
fields.description: TAP X source bit. Indicates X-axis involvement in tap event (1 = involved, 0 = not). Overwritten by new data.

fields.name: asleep
fields.description: Asleep bit. 1 = part is asleep, 0 = part is not asleep (see POWER_CTL for autosleep).

fields.name: act_z_source
fields.description: ACT Z source bit. Indicates Z-axis involvement in activity event (1 = involved, 0 = not). Overwritten by new data.

fields.name: act_y_source
fields.description: ACT Y source bit. Indicates Y-axis involvement in activity event (1 = involved, 0 = not). Overwritten by new data.

fields.name: act_x_source
fields.description: ACT X source bit. Indicates X-axis involvement in activity event (1 = involved, 0 = not). Overwritten by new data.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Always 0.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bits (fixed to 0).

fields.name: low_power
fields.description: A setting of 0 in the LOW_POWER bit selects normal operation, and a setting of 1 selects reduced power operation, which has somewhat higher noise (see the Power Modes section for details).

fields.name: rate
fields.description: These bits select the device bandwidth and output data rate (see Table 6 and Table 7 for details). The default value is 0x0A, which translates to a 100 Hz output data rate. An output data rate should be selected that is appropriate for the communication protocol and frequency selected. Selecting too high of an output data rate with a low communication speed results in samples being discarded.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved (must be 0)

fields.name: link
fields.description: Link bit: 1 links activity and inactivity functions (delays activity until inactivity detected), 0 concurrent

fields.name: auto_sleep
fields.description: AUTO_SLEEP bit: If link=1, 1 enables switch to sleep on inactivity, 0 disables

fields.name: measure
fields.description: Measure bit: 0=standby mode, 1=measurement mode

fields.name: sleep
fields.description: Sleep bit: 0=normal operation, 1=sleep mode (suppresses DATA_READY, low sampling)

fields.name: wakeup
fields.description: Wakeup bits: Control frequency of readings in sleep mode (00=8Hz, 01=4Hz, 10=2Hz, 11=1Hz)

fields.name: data_ready
fields.description: Data ready interrupt enable

fields.name: single_tap
fields.description: Single tap interrupt enable

fields.name: double_tap
fields.description: Double tap interrupt enable

fields.name: activity
fields.description: Activity interrupt enable

fields.name: inactivity
fields.description: Inactivity interrupt enable

fields.name: free_fall
fields.description: Free-fall interrupt enable

fields.name: watermark
fields.description: Watermark interrupt enable

fields.name: overrun
fields.description: Overrun interrupt enable

fields.name: data_ready
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: single_tap
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: double_tap
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: activity
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: inactivity
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: free_fall
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: watermark
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: overrun
fields.description: 0: interrupt to INT1 pin, 1: interrupt to INT2 pin

fields.name: data_ready
fields.description: Set when new data is available

fields.name: single_tap
fields.description: Set when a single tap event occurs

fields.name: double_tap
fields.description: Set when a double tap event occurs

fields.name: activity
fields.description: Set when activity greater than threshold is experienced

fields.name: inactivity
fields.description: Set when inactivity less than threshold for specified time

fields.name: free_fall
fields.description: Set when free-fall conditions are met

fields.name: watermark
fields.description: Set when FIFO samples equal specified value

fields.name: overrun
fields.description: Set when new data replaces unread data

fields.name: self_test
fields.description: A setting of 1 applies a self-test force to the sensor, causing a shift in the output data. A value of 0 disables the self-test force.

fields.name: spi
fields.description: A value of 1 sets the device to 3-wire SPI mode, and a value of 0 sets the device to 4-wire SPI mode.

fields.name: int_invert
fields.description: A value of 0 sets the interrupts to active high, and a value of 1 sets the interrupts to active low.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bit, fixed at 0.

fields.name: full_res
fields.description: When set to 1, the device is in full resolution mode (4 mg/LSB scale factor). When 0, the device is in 10-bit mode.

fields.name: justify
fields.description: A setting of 1 selects left (MSB) justified mode, and a setting of 0 selects right justified mode with sign extension.

fields.name: x_data_lsb
fields.description: Least significant byte of the X-axis acceleration output data (twos complement format)

fields.name: ydata_lsb
fields.description: Least significant byte of Y-axis acceleration data (two's complement format, controlled by DATA_FORMAT register)

fields.name: zdata0
fields.description: Least significant byte of Z-axis acceleration output data (twos complement format)

fields.name: data_z_15_8_
fields.description: Z-Axis Data MSB (twos complement format)

fields.name: fifo_mode
fields.description: Sets the FIFO mode: 00 = Bypass (FIFO bypassed), 01 = FIFO (collects up to 32 values then stops), 10 = Stream (holds last 32 values, overwrites old data), 11 = Trigger (holds samples before trigger, then collects until full).

fields.name: trigger
fields.description: Trigger interrupt select in trigger mode: 0 = INT1, 1 = INT2.

fields.name: samples
fields.description: Samples count (0 to 31). Function depends on FIFO mode: Bypass=none, FIFO/Stream=watermark trigger level, Trigger=samples retained before trigger. Value 0 sets watermark immediately.

fields.name: fifo_trig
fields.description: A 1 in the FIFO_TRIG bit corresponds to a trigger event occurring, and a 0 means that a FIFO trigger event has not occurred.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved bit, always reads as 0.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: These bits report how many data values are stored in FIFO.
